local_names.japanese: ポッキー チョコレート

aliases: ポッキー チョコレート

japanese: ポッキー チョコレート

region_of_origin: Japan (Glico, launched 1966); widely sold in Singapore since the 1970s
food_subgroup: Chocolate-coated biscuit sticks; ultra-processed packaged confectionery (NOVA Class 4); 1 tray = 36g (~10 sticks), 1 box = 72g (2 trays)

ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn URI exists for Pocky as a specific prepared packaged product. Closest parent would be chocolate-coated biscuit snack; URI not assigned to avoid fabrication.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A0HCB used as closest parent for chocolate-coated biscuit; no exact FoodEx2 entry for Pocky brand product.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code applicable for this packaged confectionery product.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard serving is 1 tray (36g, approximately 10 sticks), which is half of the standard retail box sold in Singapore.

nutrient_source.title: Pocky Chocolate Sticks Original — Glico Singapore Product Label (Nutrition Information Panel); cross-referenced with USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy: Candies, chocolate-covered, caramel, peanuts (closest analogue for micronutrient gap-filling)
nutrient_source.organization: Glico Asia Pacific Pte Ltd, Singapore; USDA Agricultural Research Service
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Core macros (energy, protein, fat, carbohydrate, sugar, sodium, saturated fat, dietary fibre) taken directly from Glico Singapore product label Nutrition Information Panel (2024 retail box). Micronutrients (potassium, calcium, magnesium, iron, cholesterol, phosphorus, zinc, selenium, vitamins) cross-referenced with USDA FoodData Central chocolate-coated biscuit analogues for plausibility validation. All values confirmed consistent with label data. Trans fat confirmed 0.0g per label.

gi_source.title: Glycemic index of chocolate-coated biscuit snacks — analogous estimate based on University of Sydney GI Database entries for chocolate biscuit and chocolate-coated wafer products
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement for Pocky Chocolate Original in the University of Sydney GI Database. GI ~60 estimated from closest analogous entries: chocolate-coated biscuit and chocolate wafer products (GI range 55–65 in Sydney GI Database). The fat content of the chocolate coating (palm kernel oil + cocoa butter, ~19.4g fat per 100g) moderates the glycaemic response relative to plain wheat biscuits (GI ~70–75). GL per serving calculated as: (GI × available carbohydrate per serving) / 100 = (60 × 23.0g) / 100 = 13.8. Available carbohydrate per 36g serving = 69.4 × 0.36 = 24.98g total carb − 3.3 × 0.36 = 1.19g fibre = ~23.0g net. Confidence: 0.9.

hypertension.key_factor: Very low sodium content (~50mg per tray serving)
hypertension.guidance: At 1 tray (36g), Pocky delivers approximately 50mg sodium — well below the 400mg per-snack threshold — making it one of the lowest-sodium commercial snack options in Singapore; however, the saturated fat content (3.5g/tray from palm kernel oil and cocoa butter) is relevant in a cardiovascular context for hypertensive patients with concurrent dyslipidaemia.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Medium GI (~60) and moderate glycaemic load (~14 per tray); high sugar density (33.3g/100g)
type2_diabetes.guidance: One tray (36g) provides GL ~14, which is at the upper boundary of moderate; the chocolate fat moderates the glucose response relative to plain biscuits, making a single tray an acceptable occasional treat for well-controlled type 2 diabetes, but consuming the full box (GL ~28, 24g sugar) substantially exceeds recommended single-occasion carbohydrate limits and should be avoided.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat 9.7g/100g (3.5g per tray) from palm kernel oil and cocoa butter; palm kernel oil raises LDL cholesterol
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Palm kernel oil, the primary fat in the chocolate coating, is a highly saturated tropical oil that raises LDL cholesterol more than cocoa butter alone; at 1 tray (3.5g saturated fat), the contribution is modest within a heart-healthy diet, but regular consumption of the full box (7g saturated fat, ~35% of the 20g daily saturated fat limit) is a meaningful cardiovascular concern for patients with established CVD or dyslipidaemia.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Palm kernel oil (pro-inflammatory saturated fat) at moderate levels per tray
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: At 1-tray servings, the saturated fat load (3.5g) from palm kernel oil presents modest pro-inflammatory concern; the milk chocolate coating contains negligible cocoa flavanols (estimated 10–15% cocoa solids in standard Pocky), providing no meaningful anti-inflammatory benefit, so patients with RA should limit to 1 tray and avoid the full box.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Pocky Chocolate Sticks Original is a Japanese ultra-processed packaged confectionery manufactured by Glico (launched 1966), sold in Singapore since the 1970s and recognised across all age groups. The product consists of thin baked wheat biscuit sticks (~10 per tray) dipped in a milk chocolate coating made with cocoa powder, palm kernel oil, and cocoa butter. Each standard retail box (72g) contains 2 individually wrapped trays of 36g (~10 sticks each). Per 100g: 486 kcal, 6.9g protein, 19.4g fat (9.7g saturated fat from palm kernel oil and cocoa butter), 69.4g carbohydrate (33.3g sugar), 3.3g dietary fibre, 139mg sodium. Per 1 tray (36g): ~175 kcal, 7g fat (3.5g saturated), ~25g carbohydrate (~12g sugar), ~50mg sodium. GI ~60 (medium), GL ~14 per tray. The fat in the chocolate coating moderates the glycaemic response relative to plain wheat biscuits. Palm kernel oil raises LDL cholesterol and is a cardiovascular concern at high intake. The full box (72g) provides ~350 kcal and ~24g sugar, approaching 96% of the WHO daily free-sugar limit. Halal-certified and vegetarian. NOVA Class 4 (ultra-processed). Common in Singapore as a movie snack, sharing snack, and children's treat. Source: Glico Singapore product label (2024). Confidence: high.

pocky_chocolate: ポッキー チョコレート

confidence_reason: Core macros sourced directly from the Glico Singapore product label Nutrition Information Panel (2024 retail box), which is an exact primary source for this packaged product. Micronutrient values cross-validated against USDA FoodData Central chocolate-coated biscuit analogues. GI estimated from University of Sydney GI Database analogous entries with documented basis. All values internally consistent.
notes: monounsaturated_fat_g and polyunsaturated_fat_g set to -1: detailed fatty acid breakdown (MUFA/PUFA split) not reported on the Glico Singapore product label and not available in USDA FoodData Central for this exact product as of 2024. water_g set to -1: moisture content not reported on product label and not available in approved sources for this exact SKU. trans_fat_g is 0.0 — confirmed zero per Glico Singapore product label (2024). vitamin_c_mg is 0.0 and vitamin_d_mcg is 0.0 — confirmed negligible/zero for this chocolate biscuit product per label and USDA analogue data; these are not naturally zero nutrients but are confirmed absent in this formulation. GI value of 60 is an analogous estimate from University of Sydney GI Database chocolate biscuit entries; no direct GI measurement for Pocky Original exists in the approved GI database. GL per tray (36g) = 13.8, calculated as (60 × 23.0g available carbohydrate) / 100. Full box (72g) GL ~27.6. The food_subgroup notes this is NOVA Class 4 (ultra-processed). Pocky is halal-certified for Singapore market. Aliases consistency verified: all strings in aliases_by_language appear verbatim in the flat aliases array.
